identity.name: Activity
identity.definition: An Activity is a discrete, interactive element embedded within a Nearpod Lesson that presents a prompt or question to students, collects their responses, and optionally produces a score.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How an Activity appears in lesson builders and activity grids

attributes.name: Title/Label
attributes.description: Display name shown in lesson editor and during delivery.

attributes.name: Activity Type
attributes.description: Determines UI template and response collection method. Quiz, Poll, Open-Ended, Draw It, Matching Pairs, Fill in Blank, Collaborate Board, Memory Test, Time to Climb, Drag and Drop.

attributes.name: Point Value
attributes.description: Points awarded for correct response. Only applicable when scoring is enabled.

representations.heading: List layout
representations.description: How an Activity appears in lesson activity lists and reorder views

attributes.name: Position
attributes.description: Order within the lesson. Determines sequence during delivery.

attributes.name: Title/Label
attributes.description: Display name shown in lesson editor and during delivery.

attributes.name: Activity Type
attributes.description: Determines UI template and response collection method.

representations.heading: Detail page layout
representations.description: Full Activity view during configuration or results review

examples.title: Vocabulary Match
examples.questionPrompt: Match each vocabulary word with its correct definition.

attributes.name: Title/Label
attributes.description: Display name shown in lesson editor and during delivery.

attributes.name: Activity Type
attributes.description: Determines UI template and response collection method.

attributes.name: Question/Prompt
attributes.description: The question or instruction presented to students.

attributes.name: Time Limit
attributes.description: Optional time constraint in seconds for timed activities.

attributes.name: Response Count
attributes.description: Total number of student responses collected.

attributes.name: Average Score
attributes.description: Mean score across all student responses (percentage).

stories.title: Add Activity to Lesson
stories.benefit: I can make my lesson interactive and check for student understanding at key moments.
stories.whenClause: I insert an activity into my lesson,
stories.thenClause: it appears in the lesson timeline at the specified position and is ready for configuration.

stories.title: Launch Activity During Session
stories.benefit: students see the prompt on their devices and can submit responses in real time.
stories.whenClause: I advance to an activity slide during a live session,
stories.thenClause: the activity is presented to all connected students and responses begin streaming in.

stories.title: Respond to Activity
stories.benefit: my answer is recorded and I can see if I was correct (for auto-scored activities).
stories.whenClause: I submit my response to an activity,
stories.thenClause: my response is saved, the response count increments, and a Score is generated if scoring is enabled.

businessRules.title: Lesson Containment
businessRules.description: An Activity must belong to exactly one Lesson. It cannot exist independently outside a Lesson context.

businessRules.title: Type Immutability
businessRules.description: Once an Activity is created with a specific type (e.g., Quiz, Poll), the type cannot be changed. The teacher must delete and recreate to switch types.

businessRules.title: Scoring Dependency
businessRules.description: Point Value and Correct Answer fields are only editable when Scoring Method is set to 'auto' or 'manual'. They are hidden when scoring is 'none'.

businessRules.title: Answer Options
businessRules.description: Answer Options are required for Quiz, Matching Pairs, and Fill in Blank types. They are not applicable to Open-Ended, Draw It, or Collaborate Board types.

businessRules.title: Position Uniqueness
businessRules.description: Each Activity's Position must be unique within its parent Lesson. Reordering recalculates positions for all sibling activities.

businessRules.title: Response Finality
businessRules.description: Once a student submits a response during a Live Session, it cannot be modified. Self-paced assignments may allow resubmission based on teacher configuration.

businessRules.title: Auto-Scoring
businessRules.description: Activities with Scoring Method 'auto' are graded immediately upon submission. The system compares the student response against the Correct Answer.

businessRules.title: Time Limit Enforcement
businessRules.description: If a Time Limit is set, the activity auto-submits when time expires. Unanswered activities receive zero points.

businessRules.title: Duplication
businessRules.description: Duplicating an Activity copies all configuration (type, prompt, answers, scoring) but resets Response Count and Average Score to zero.

businessRules.title: Lifecycle Inheritance
businessRules.description: An Activity inherits its lifecycle state from its parent Lesson. A draft Lesson means all its activities are in draft.

businessRules.title: Average Score Calculation
businessRules.description: Average Score is recalculated each time a new response is submitted. It represents the mean percentage across all student responses.

businessRules.title: Skip Behavior
businessRules.description: When a teacher skips an Activity during a Live Session, no responses are collected and the Activity does not contribute to the session report.

states.name: Draft
states.description: Activity is being configured within a draft lesson
states.triggers: Created when teacher adds activity to lesson

states.name: Published
states.description: Activity is part of a published lesson, ready for delivery

states.name: Launched
states.description: Activity is currently being presented in a live session
states.triggers: Teacher advances to this activity during live delivery

states.name: Closed
states.description: Activity has been completed or skipped, responses are final
states.triggers: Teacher advances past activity or session ends

relationships.mechanics: Activities are created within a Lesson by the teacher using the lesson editor. Each Activity is positioned within the lesson's slide sequence.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. An Activity belongs to exactly one Lesson; a Lesson contains zero or more Activities.
relationships.sorts: By position within lesson (default), by activity type, by scoring method.
relationships.filters: By activity type, by scoring method (auto/manual/none), by completion status.
relationships.dependencies: If a Lesson is deleted, all contained Activities are deleted. If a Lesson is duplicated, all Activities are duplicated with reset metrics.

relationships.mechanics: Scores are generated when a student responds to a scored Activity. Auto-scored activities produce Scores immediately; manually-scored activities produce Scores when the teacher grades them.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. An Activity generates many Scores (one per student response); each Score references exactly one Activity.
relationships.sorts: By submission time (default), by student name, by score value.
relationships.filters: By score range, by student, by correct/incorrect.
relationships.dependencies: If an Activity is removed from a Lesson, associated Scores from completed sessions persist for historical reporting.

relationships.mechanics: Activities are presented during a Live Session when the teacher advances to the activity slide. The session controls the delivery timing.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many (via Lesson). An Activity can be delivered across multiple Live Sessions of the same Lesson; a Live Session delivers all Activities in its Lesson.
relationships.filters: By launched/skipped status, by response count.
relationships.dependencies: A Live Session must reference a Lesson to access its Activities. If an Activity is skipped, it is excluded from the session report.

nestedObjects.name: Score
nestedObjects.description: Generated when students respond to the activity

allAttributes.name: Activity Type
allAttributes.description: Determines UI template and response method. Values: quiz, poll, open-ended, draw-it, matching-pairs, fill-in-blank, collaborate-board, memory-test, time-to-climb, drag-and-drop.

allAttributes.name: Title/Label
allAttributes.description: Display name shown in lesson editor and during delivery.

allAttributes.name: Question/Prompt
allAttributes.description: The question or instruction presented to students.

allAttributes.name: Answer Options
allAttributes.description: Set of possible answers. Required for quiz, matching-pairs, and fill-in-blank types.

allAttributes.name: Correct Answer
allAttributes.description: The expected correct response. Required when scoring method is auto.

allAttributes.name: Scoring Method
allAttributes.description: How the activity is graded. Values: auto, manual, none.

allAttributes.name: Time Limit
allAttributes.description: Optional time constraint in seconds for timed activities.

allAttributes.name: Position
allAttributes.description: Order within the parent lesson. Unique per lesson.

allAttributes.name: Point Value
allAttributes.description: Points awarded for correct response. Only applicable when scoring is enabled.

allAttributes.name: Response Count
allAttributes.description: Total number of student responses collected across all sessions.

allAttributes.name: Average Score
allAttributes.description: Mean score percentage across all student responses.

structure.evidence: An Activity has a rich attribute set: type, title, question/prompt, answer options, scoring method, point value, time limit, and position. It supports a full configuration page.

instances.evidence: A single Nearpod lesson typically contains 5-15 activities. 'Vocabulary Match' (matching-pairs, auto-scored), 'Exit Ticket: Main Idea' (open-ended, manually-scored), 'Quick Poll: How confident are you?' (poll, unscored).

purpose.evidence: Activities are the core engagement mechanism in Nearpod. Teachers configure them, students respond to them, and reports aggregate their results. They drive the interactive nature of every Nearpod lesson.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain object within the Nearpod content ecosystem. The atomic unit of interactivity within a Lesson.

synonyms.notes: Students see activities as questions or prompts
Often used as a shorthand for auto-scored activities
Emphasizes the interactive nature vs. static slides
Frames activities as formative assessment checkpoints

objectViews.description: Activities within a lesson being authored, shown as draggable cards with scoring and point settings. Authors configure, preview, reorder, or duplicate activities while building a lesson.
The active activity during a live session, displayed full-screen with real-time response counts. Teachers launch activities, monitor participation, and control pacing.
Activities listed with response counts and average scores in a lesson report. Teachers review which activities students struggled with or excelled at.
